known_bugs.description: Duplicate leading slashes stripped in URI paths (PR #7315)

tp_classification.note: None of the 4 known bugs were explicitly detected. Subagent focused on cookie handling (P0: toReturn None check) and general code quality issues rather than the security/network-layer bugs in KB 1-4.

false_negatives.why_missed: Subagent audited utils.py but did not flag extract_zipped_paths zip extraction logic. Likely requires security-specific lens (v0.3 P2.8' architecture lens would help).
Subagent checked models.py but did not trace Response.history assignment chain. Requires deep object lifecycle analysis.
no_proxy logic in utils.py was not flagged. Requires network-domain-specific knowledge.
URI path normalization in utils.py not identified as bug-prone. String manipulation pattern not captured by generic subagent prompt.

analysis.strengths: Subagent found a legitimate P0 bug (cookies.py:411 null-check issue) and 18 other quality issues. Precision is 84% — most findings are real code quality problems, just not the known bugs we were targeting.
analysis.weaknesses: 0/4 recall for known bugs. All 4 FNs are in the security/networking domain (path traversal exploit, proxy bypass, URI normalization, object lifecycle). ADD's generic subagent prompt is biased toward code quality patterns (null checks, type hints, exception handling) rather than security/network-layer contracts.
analysis.root_cause: The benchmark's known bugs are all security-adjacent. ADD v0.2.2's subagent prompt covers general engineering principles but has no security-specific guidance. This is the same gap that the security-best-practices skill fills with its 13-step audit checklist and language-specific reference files.
